perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties agrees that the Information shall not be exported directly or indirectly to any restricted or prohibited country without the prior written consent of the Bureau of Industry & Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce, where such consent is required to be obtained. Each Party acknowledges the other Party’s potential obligations under the federal securities laws, but you will first consult with the such other Party regarding the timing and content of such disclosure and otherwise comply with the terms and provisions of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties agrees that the Information shall be exported directly or indirectly to any restricted or prohibited country, at each parties sole discretion, regardless of the need for consent of the Bureau of Industry & Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce, where such consent is required to be obtained. Each Party acknowledges the other Party’s potential obligations under the federal securities laws, but you will first consult with the such other Party regarding the timing and content of such disclosure and otherwise comply with the terms and provisions of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 11 requires prior written consent from the Bureau of Industry & Security for exporting information to restricted countries, whereas the change removes that restriction and gives sole discretion to each party, contradicting the original requirement. This creates uncertainty as to whether the parties need to comply with export regulations or if they can bypass them at their discretion. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Parties agrees that the Information shall be exported directly or indirectly to any restricted or prohibited country, at each parties sole discretion, regardless of the need for consent of the Bureau of Industry & Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce, where such consent is required to be obtained. Each Party acknowledges the other Party’s potential obligations under the federal securities laws, but you will first consult with the such other Party regarding the timing and content of such disclosure and otherwise comply with the terms and provisions of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires consent from the Bureau of Industry & Security for exporting information to restricted countries. The changed text removes this requirement, granting each party sole discretion to export regardless of the need for consent. This directly contradicts the original obligation to obtain consent, creating a clear inconsistency and uncertainty about compliance with export regulations. The contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, meaning that this changed text creates a contradiction with the previous requirement.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it will apply for and obtain intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information at will.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 6 explicitly prohibits either party from seeking intellectual property protection for the other party's information. The modified text mandates that both parties must now apply for IP protection, creating a direct contradiction. This introduces uncertainty about ownership rights and the parties' obligations regarding IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of complying with the obligations set forth herein, each of COMPANY and XPLORE shall use efforts fully commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its privileged and confidential Information. Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party. Each Party agrees that it will apply for and obtain intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information at will. All intellectual property rights relating to any drawings, documents and work carried out by any Party (whether past, present or future) using the other Party’s Information will belong to and will vest in the other Party. Each Party agrees that it will do all such things and execute all documents necessary to enable the other Party to obtain, defend or enforce its rights in such drawings, documents and work.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party would apply for intellectual property protection regarding the other party's information. The changed text mandates that each party *will* apply for and obtain intellectual property protection for the other party's Information. This directly contradicts the meaning and intent of the original clause and causes a clear and direct conflict in the document, especially within the provided contradicted text section that states 'all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party' and repeats the changed text. This creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices regardless of regulatory purposes or legal process.
perturbation.explanation: Section 12 originally allows for retention of electronic backups of COMPANY Information under certain conditions, explicitly stating they should not be used or disclosed unless required by law. The modified text now states that XPLORE is 'required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is reasonably practical' which contradicts the 'will not be required to destroy' part, and that any retained backup information 'shall be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices regardless of regulatory purposes or legal process', directly contradicting the restriction on usage/disclosure. This contradiction creates uncertainty about how XPLORE should handle backups of COMPANY Information, and whether they must actively destroy or can use it regardless of legality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Upon the request of COMPANY, XPLORE will return or destroy (at XPLORE’S option) all COMPANY Information and any notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information, including all copies thereof, then in the possession of XPLORE or its Representatives. Such return, however, does not abrogate the continuing obligations of XPLORE under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices regardless of regulatory purposes or legal process. Any destruction will be certified by an officer of XPLORE, and all retained information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original purpose and restrictions regarding the use and destruction of COMPANY Information backups. Originally, XPLORE was *not* required to destroy backups if impractical, and any retained backups were *not* to be used or disclosed unless legally required. The modified text *requires* destruction when reasonably practical and allows retained backups to be used and disclosed *regardless* of legal requirements. This directly conflicts with the 'COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices regardless of regulatory purposes or legal process.' from the contradicted section creating a strong in-text contradiction and makes it ambiguous if XPLORE is supposed to destroy COMPANY Information backups or use/disclose them freely.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of such dispute or proceeding shall be heard and determined in such courts (and the courts hearing appeals from such courts). The Parties hereby irrevocably waive, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any objection which they may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any such dispute brought in such court or any defense of inconvenient forum in connection therewith. Each Party hereto waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any right it may have to a trial by jury in respect of any suit, action or other proceeding arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of such dispute or proceeding shall be heard and determined in such courts (and the courts hearing appeals from such courts). The Parties hereby reserve their right to object to the laying of venue of any such dispute brought in such court or any defense of inconvenient forum in connection therewith. Each Party hereto reserves their right to a trial by jury in respect of any suit, action or other proceeding arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 15 contains a waiver of the right to object to venue and a waiver of the right to a jury trial. The modification negates these waivers by explicitly stating that the parties 'reserve' these rights. This creates a direct contradiction as the agreement both waives and reserves these legal protections, which introduces legal ambiguities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws. The Parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Delaware and the federal courts of the United States of America located in the State of Delaware over any dispute, claim or matter arising out of or relating to the Agreement. Each Party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of such dispute or proceeding shall be heard and determined in such courts (and the courts hearing appeals from such courts). The Parties hereby reserve their right to object to the laying of venue of any such dispute brought in such court or any defense of inconvenient forum in connection therewith. Each Party hereto reserves their right to a trial by jury in respect of any suit, action or other proceeding arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a waiver of the right to object to venue and a jury trial. The changed text explicitly reserves these rights. The contradicted text now contains both a waiver and a reservation of these rights, creating a direct and undeniable contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause regarding waiving/reserving rights is placed in the same location with conflicting implications. The parties cannot simultaneously waive and reserve the same rights. This would introduce significant legal ambiguity and make the contract unenforceable on these points.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that it and all of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted an unlimited non-exclusive license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6 states that no license or right is granted by either party concerning the other's information. The modified text states the opposite: that each party IS granted an 'unlimited non-exclusive license', creating an in-text contradiction. This change undermines the original intent of the agreement and opens the door to legal disputes over the permitted use of the provided confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of complying with the obligations set forth herein, each of COMPANY and XPLORE shall use efforts fully commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its privileged and confidential Information. Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party agrees that it and all of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted an unlimited non-exclusive license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party. Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information. All intellectual property rights relating to any drawings, documents and work carried out by any Party (whether past, present or future) using the other Party’s Information will belong to and will vest in the other Party. Each Party agrees that it will do all such things and execute all documents necessary to enable the other Party to obtain, defend or enforce its rights in such drawings, documents and work.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party has been granted any license, copyright, or similar right. The changed text states that each party HAS been granted an unlimited non-exclusive license, copyright, or similar right. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating a clear conflict. The contradicted text contains the same conflicting statements, because the changed text was modified in the contradicted text. This meets the definition of a Structural Flaw, which causes in-text contradictions when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
